title: Branding: Personal Or Strictly Business | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: Branding： Personal Or Strictly Business ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So the next question is branding personal or strictly business [Music] some people have a mix and they like it mainly business stuff with a few personal bits snapped in to give it a sense of and others have no personal stuff and others go too far and post all their personal stuff…

segments.text: so the next question is branding personal or strictly business [Music] some people have a mix and they like it mainly business stuff with a few personal bits snapped in to give it a sense of and others have no personal stuff and others go too far and post all their personal stuff like pictures of their food so what's the right balance well it depends a lot on the channel and the brand that you're setting up the first thing you've got to do is know your audience and i actually think the way social media is going is that the personal stuff is really important because otherwise you look a little bit corporate and if you're a corporate it's hard to build one of the three pillars of trust i always talk about trust in terms of three pillars and the one that i'm talking about is the sense of emotional safety so that means that you can walk into a room and feel comfortable with someone it's very hard to do that if they're very corporate if you've only got before and after pictures with very little of your opinion or your spirit in them it makes it hard to imagine what

you might be like when you get there and you might be wonderful but uncertainty creates hesitation in people so they simply won't call until they feel like yeah that's the kind of person i could tell my deepest darkest insecurities to for example so um you do need a degree of vulnerability i would say um and then of course there are other reasons to be personal obviously the cliche with instagram is that you post pictures of yourself living the perfect life and that is um engaging for people that people find it aspirational but without a little bit of vulnerability in there as well it can make you feel unaccessible so the best combination is that they want to they feel like you understand the secret of how to live a happy life they can buy some of that for you but also you're a normal person just like them and without those elements um you don't come across as someone that they can imagine having a conversation with so that's what i'd suggest is that mixture they need to know what you do and that you're good at it that's what most people do

and reasonably well without thinking about too much but the personal stuff is what builds trust in a different way so i do think you should have a mix of it and it's up for you what you feel comfortable with there are people who've built millions of followers without ever showing a picture of any of their kids you can do that but if you feel comfortable doing that occasionally it's fine i think a lot of people hide behind their kids and miranda actually told me this is my wife she's very interested in personal branding and she uh she pointed out that for a long time she was doing that you feel comfortable putting a picture of someone else on social media especially when they're cute and gorgeous because they're your kids and but actually what people want to know is you're the main character how are you engaging with the with life and with your business and so that they can make an assessment whether they'd like to be seen by you or not effectively that's what it is so a degree of vulnerability is important and that means you've got to be a little bit personal just don't be boring the final thing i would say is whatever you are don't be beige don't be boring the stuff that you're most afraid people will spot about you your quirkiness is probably the only thing that differentiates you from everyone else so

i always say be real and because that's the only thing you can be if you're not being real you're basically being a cardboard cutout and no one wants to see a cardboard cutout be yourself even if you're imperfect it's probably the only thing that differentiates you from the next person
